prompt: The expensive fallback is always called even when the Optional has a value. What is the bug?
choices: orElse only evaluates its argument when the Optional is empty

orElse(T other) evaluates the argument expression eagerly before calling orElse; expensive() is always called regardless of whether the Optional has a value; use orElseGet(() -> expensive()) to evaluate lazily
Optional.of("value") is incorrect; use Optional.ofNullable to wrap non-null values
orElse must be replaced with or() to support supplier-style fallbacks
explanation: orElse(T other) receives an already-evaluated value — the argument is computed before orElse is even called, just like any method argument in Java. expensive() always runs. Fix: opt.orElseGet(() -> expensive()) wraps the call in a Supplier so it is only invoked when the Optional is empty.
